channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Microsoft hired an analyst with an influential video game blog to fix Xbox
item.description: Matthew Ball is the new chief strategy officer for Xbox ahead of Project Helix.

item.title: The SpaceX IPO filing is filled with AI bets, Starship dreams, and Elon Musk at the center
item.description: SpaceX has finally made the contents of its IPO filing public, weeks ahead of what is expected to be the largest IPO ever and one that will make Musk the CEO, CTO, and Chairman of the board.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Thursday, May 21 (game #1075)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Thursday, May 21 (game #1578)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Thursday, May 21 (game #809)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Clouted wants to take the guesswork out of making short videos go viral
item.description: The video clipping startup raised a $7 million seed round led by Slow Ventures.

item.title: Google's Managed Agents API promises one-call deployment at the cost of execution layer control
item.description: At Google I/O, the company unveiled Managed Agents in its Gemini API — a service that promises to collapse weeks of agent deployment work into a single API call. It's also a sign that Google believes its ecosystem, including the newly launched Antigravity CLI, is ready to own the execution layer end-to-end.Before a single agent is written, teams are already spending days on the unglamorous work: standing up execution environments, managing sandboxes, wiring tool call infrastructure. Model providers like Anthropic have launched platforms to handle much of that work — but Google's approach is di...

item.title: xAI burned $6.4B last year — SpaceX’s IPO filing shows why the spending is far from over
item.description: SpaceX's IPO filing reveals xAI lost $6.4 billion in 2025 while planning a massive Grok expansion — offering the first public look at Elon Musk's AI financials and more details about his ambitions.

item.title: The Best Outdoor Deals From the REI Anniversary Sale 2026
item.description: It’s the best time of year to pick up all the outdoor gadgets, tents, sleeping bags, and other gear you’ll need for summer fun.

item.title: Nvidia posts another record quarter, reveals $43B of holdings in startups
item.description: Nvidia announced another record revenue figure after market close on Wednesday, but forecasted that revenue growth would slow in the following quarter.

item.title: We may only have a year of the RAM crisis left if this ex-Samsung boss is right
item.description: Ex-Samsung exec says it's possible the RAM crisis 'will change starting from the second half of next year' as supply and demand begin to correct.

item.title: Hulu bundle subscribers can now access their watch history and recs in the Disney+ app
item.description: Even though Disney really wants you in its own app, it isn't killing off Hulu.

item.title: A Bipartisan Amendment Would End Police License Plate Tracking Nationwide
item.description: One line tucked into a federal highway bill would strip funds from cities and states unless they kill their automated plate tracking programs—effectively banning the tech for all but toll collection.

item.title: Musk’s xAI is being sued over its data center generators — now it’s buying $2.8B more
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI said it will buy $2.8 billion worth of natural gas turbines over the next three years, according to SpaceX's IPO filing.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Filing Reveals Anthropic Is Paying $15 Billion a Year to Access Its Data Centers
item.description: The long-awaited documents SpaceX filed with US regulators Wednesday included details about a lucrative deal to lend GPUs to a major AI rival.

item.title: The SpaceX IPO plans are now public
item.description: The paperwork sheds new light on the company that will soon trade as SPCX.

item.title: What 21,000 design submissions taught me about sustainability
item.description: Design has moved from aspiration to expectation. For the past two years, I’ve had a front-row seat to one of the largest and most rigorous datasets in global design. Each year, the iF DESIGN AWARD receives over 10,000 submissions spanning 93 categories of design. Participants include industry giants like Apple and Coca-Cola, to startups and independent design studios that are actively shaping the field’s future. Taken together, these entries offer more than a snapshot of excellence. They reveal where design is actually headed....

item.title: Why a Murdoch buying Vox and New York Magazine might not be like a bad episode of ‘Succession’
item.description: James Murdoch’s private equity firm and Vox may seem like an odd fit, but this time it might actually be different. Here’s why. The sale of a marquee digital media company on Succession, the HBO series that ran from 2018-2023 , was always going to end badly.

item.title: Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25B per month for compute
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI surprised the AI world when it made a deal to sell compute to Anthropic. Now we know how much it's worth.

item.title: SpaceX just filed for what could be the biggest IPO ever
item.description: Elon Musk's final frontier is officially open for business now that SpaceX has formally filed its S-1 prospectus with the SEC. That kicks off what could be the largest initial public offering ever when it lists on the Nasdaq stock exchange with the ticker SPCX. SpaceX generated $18.67 billion in revenue in 2025, driven largely […]
